item.title: [BD-4660] Why Are React Native Deep Links Not Working?
item.description:
Migration request: warn users of some potential issues they may run into when attempting to integrate deep links in their React Native build{}

Knowledge gap: Our docs do not mention what is mentioned in the "Resolution" section of the KA.

Recommended actions: In the KA, at the very end there is a line "if that does not solve the issue, further troubleshooting will be required" which might come off as a little vague, and maybe they should instead write in to Support

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/push_notifications/?tab=ios%20native&sdktab=react%20native

item.title: [BD-4651] Maximum Recipient Limit is not Respected for a Campaign
item.description:
1. Migration request: 
    1. Explain to users why adjusting a rate limiting after a campaign has launched can impact the send metrics and not align with rate limit being set
  2. Knowledge gap: 
    1. it helps customers know possible scenario for misalignment between send data and max recipient.
  3. Recommended actions: 
    1. Add an additional FAQ to the docs outlining that making changes to max recipient to an active Campaign may cause misalignment with send analytics . If the maximum recipient limit was added post-launch, the campaign may have been respecting the rule at the time of the send. If the recipient limit is changed after the messages are enqueued, the original rate limit would be applied. If the sends are also rate limited, this could lead to some unexpected behavior.
  4. Flag sensitive information: None{}
  5. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): 
    1. https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/engagement_tools/campaigns/faq/#how-do-i-create-a-multichannel-campaign
item.summary: Maximum Recipient Limit is not Respected for a Campaign

item.title: [BD-4597] How to Log a Custom Event Using GTM (Google Tag Manager)
item.description:
Migration request: Provide instructions for how to Log a Custom Event using Google Tag Manager (GTM)

Knowledge gap: Our public docs don’t provide clear instructions / examples for how to log a Custom Event with GTM.  Our docs say that our customers can use the Braze Action tag to log analytics, but clicking "logging analytics" links out to a broken page (404). This makes it difficult for our customers to see any specific instructions or examples.

Recommended actions: Add a section (or a new page) that outlines how to log a Custom Event with GTM, including an example. The Knowledge Article includes the steps

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):

item.title: [BD-4541] Catalogs use cases Vs Catalogs Selections use cases
item.description:
Knowledge Request

  1. Migration Request:
    • Enhance the Braze documentation regarding Catalogs and Catalog Selections to include guidance on structuring Catalog data based on end use cases and the differences between standard Catalog data and Catalog Selections.
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation does not provide clear guidance on how to effectively structure Catalog data for various use cases, nor does it explain the differences between using standard Catalog calls and Catalog Selections.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation to include:
      • Structuring Your Catalog Data in Braze:
        • When planning how to structure your Catalog data in Braze, it's important to think backwards from your end use case. Each row in the Catalog refers to an “item” (with a unique ID). The columns in the Catalog should contain information relating to that “item,” such as URLs, description copy, image URLs, price, rating, size, color, etc.
      • Catalogs:
        • With standard Catalog calls, you match a value against the ID column. By dynamically inserting a Custom Attribute or Event Property (in the form of an ID string) into the Catalog call, you can pull multiple pieces of information relating to one item (or row) a user has engaged with into the message for personalization. This opens up use cases such as:
          • Recent product/service viewed ID
          • Wishlist items ID
          • Deals by location ID
          • Product purchased ID
          • Lifecycle stage ID
          • Last searched product/service ID
          • Content by days since sign up
      • Catalog Selections:
        • Another way to leverage your Catalog data is through the Selections feature. After creating your Catalog, you can apply a series of filters to the data in your other columns to run queries against the Catalog and return up to 10 items (rows) that meet the specified criteria. To make the items returned personal to a user, insert Custom Attributes or Event Properties into the filters to make this dynamic. Here, you are matching values in any of the columns in the Catalog, rather than just the ID column.
        • Depending on the data a user has logged on their profile, each user will receive a different combination of recommendations. Main use cases include:
          • Up to 10 items where category equals X
          • Brand equals X
          • Cuisine equals X
          • Size equals X
          • Subscription type equals X
          • Average order value equals X
          • Destination/location equals X
          • Any column in your table…
      • Additional Use Cases:
        • Q: Customer wants to display messages that are two days after send time. The catalog selection filters pull a time attribute, but there are no instructions on how to fill it in or no way to have it be dynamic depending on send time. Is it true that this is not possible with catalog selection either?
          • A: You can use Liquid for the value of the filter. For example, this would filter items based on today's date:

liquid
Copy
{{{{{{ 'now' | date: "%Y-%m-%d" }}}}}}

        • Use Cases NOT Supported:
          • There is a field from catalogs (e.g., Event Address) that does not populate for every single entry. If a customer wants to pull a catalog selection of every event that has an Event Address not blank, this is not supported with the catalog selection filters. There is no filter for a value not being set. Customers can submit a Product Request for this.
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):
    • By providing this additional information on structuring Catalog data and the differences between standard Catalog calls and Catalog Selections, users will have a clearer understanding of how to effectively utilize their Catalogs in Braze, leading to improved personalization and campaign performance.
  1. Flag Sensitive Information:
    • No sensitive information identified.
  1. Related PQ:
    • n/a
  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/personalization_and_dynamic_content/catalogs
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/personalization_and_dynamic_content/catalogs/selections/#use-case
item.summary: Catalogs use cases Vs Catalogs Selections use cases

item.title: [BD-4535] Submitting a “Request Timed Out” Support Ticket
item.description:
Knowledge Request

  1. Migration Request:
    • Enhance the Braze documentation regarding troubleshooting "Request Timed Out" errors to include specific information on what to include when raising a ticket to Braze Support on the Campaign FAQ page and the Canvas FAQ page
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation does not provide detailed guidance on how to effectively report "Request Timed Out" errors to Braze Support, which can hinder timely resolution of issues.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation to include:
      • Information to Include When Raising a Ticket for "Request Timed Out" Errors:
        1. Screen Recording:
          • Provide a screen recording of the steps taken before seeing the error message. This helps Braze Support reproduce the error on their end.
        2. Step-by-Step Summary:
          • Include a written summary of the exact steps taken from the very beginning before encountering the error.
        3. Timestamps:
          • Provide the exact timestamp(s) of when the error occurred, including the timezone.
        4. Link to Dashboard Page/Campaign/Canvas:
          • Include a link to the specific Dashboard page, Campaign, or Canvas where the error message appears.
        5. Frequency of the Error:
          • Outline how often the "Request Timed Out" error occurs. Is it intermittent or constant? Is it blocking any actions (e.g., launching/saving Canvases)?
        6. Browser Console Screenshot:
          • Take a screenshot of the browser console when the error message appears. To access the console, right-click anywhere within the browser window and select “Inspect.” Then, go to the “Console” tab and capture any errors or warnings that appear when the "Request Timed Out" error is displayed.
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):
    • By providing this additional information on how to report "Request Timed Out" errors, users will be better equipped to communicate issues to Braze Support, leading to quicker resolutions and improved user experience.
  1. Flag Sensitive Information:
    • No sensitive information identified.
  1. Related PQ:
    • n/a
  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
    • n/a
item.summary: Submitting a “Request Timed Out” Support Ticket

comments.comment: Isaiah Robinson Here's a one off ticket from the Salesforce migration that doesn't quite fit anything else. You recently updated this section of the documentation. Can you take a swing at this one?

item.title: [BD-4514] Testing Nested Custom Attributes and Nested Objects for Custom Event Properties
item.description:
Migration request: Explain how to test with Nested Custom Attributes and Nested Objects for Custom Event Properties

Knowledge gap: Our docs explain what Nested Custom Attributes are, and how to import them to Braze. But they don't explain how to test messaging that references a Nested Custom Attribute or Nested Objects for Custom Event Properties via liquid. Our Preview & Test tool doesn't support adding mock data for this. We should be in the habit of clearly outlining how customers should test features and encouraging them to do so - it's easier to fix a problem if it's caught in early testing than if it's post-launch

Recommended actions: Add a section called "Testing Nested Custom Attributes" and "Testing Nested Objects" to the relevant feature pages. This could be written step-by-step or a video walk-through

"Testing Nested Custom Attributes"

Prerequisites: campaign that references Nested Custom Attributes via liquid

  1. import the nested attributes to the user profile you're using to test
  2. on your campaign, navigate to "Preview and Test"
  3. preview the message as the user from step 1

"Testing Nested Objects"

Prerequisites: campaign that is triggered by or references Nested Objects, targeting your test users

  1. launch your test campaign to your test audience
  2. log the custom event with nested object to your test user's profile

Flag sensitive information: N/A

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):

  • Nested custom attributes-> add a section for "How to test with nested custom attributes"
  • Nested object in custom events -> add a section for "How to test with nested objects in custom events"
item.summary: Testing Nested Custom Attributes and Nested Objects for Custom Event Properties

item.title: [BD-4502] Push Notification Titles Appear Correctly on Android but not iOS
item.description:
1. Migration request: Add to the push troubleshooting docs.
  2. Knowledge gap: I'm not seeing this information about formatting push titles differently for Android vs iOS in our docs currently, and this feels like an important callout.
  3. Recommended actions: 

Issue
When using Liquid to send a customised push campaign, the push displays correctly and in full for Android. However, for iOS, the title seems to be cut off as soon as any Liquid variable is reached.For example: a customised title reading "Regarding your flight from Italy to France", where Italy and France are Liquid variables, appears as expected on Android.

On iOS, this would read "Regarding your flight from".

Resolution
When Android parses a string to be inserted into a push title, it automatically cleans the title of any whitespace on the left and right-hand side, tabs, all newlines, and so on (known as stripping a string).

Conversely, on iOS, newlines (\n) are not filtered out. So, if a title reads "This is a\n test title", the title will appear to be "This is a test title" on Android but "This is a" on iOS.

Rather than trying to rebuild the Liquid without including any newlines, you can use the Liquid strip_newlines filter to remove any newlines included in the string automatically. As a result, these removed newlines will not corrupt the title and the entire title may be rendered correctly in an iOS push.

One technique to more easily achieve the desired result is to wrap it with a capture command. This makes it easier to add the strip_newlines filter.

Example:
Create the capture (named x):
​​​​​​{% capture x %} YOUR LIQUID HERE {% endcapture %}Call the capture in your message, while removing the newlines:
{{ x | strip_newlines }}

  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/push/troubleshooting
item.summary: Push Notification Titles Appear Correctly on Android but not iOS

item.title: [BD-4473] Troubleshoot uploading .p12 Certificate to Braze
item.description:
Migration request:  The doc should contain that the customers may see the following warning. 

Knowledge gap: Our public docs don't outline the Warning when trying to upload the .p12 Certificate to Braze.
"Warning: The legacy .p12 and .pem certificate file types are deprecated in favor of .p8 files. Unlike .p8 files, the legacy file types expire annually and require error -prone manual updating. See https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/platform_integration_guides/ios/push_notifications/integration for assistance generating and uploading a .p8 file. If you are confident you need to use a .p12 or . pem certificate, please reach out to our support team for assistance: https://www.braze.com/docs/support_contact.""
 

Recommended actions: Using the content from the Knowledge Article, add the warning message and what it may mean: "As mentioned in the warning, .p12 files require error-prone manual updating and our customers should be aware of this before proceeding further. "

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): page: https://braze.com/docs/user_guide/personalization_and_dynamic_content/liquid/liquid_use_cases
item.summary: Troubleshoot uploading .p12 Certificate to Braze

item.title: [BD-4460] The Difference Between "Send to Production" and "Send to Development" When Adding an Apple Push Certificate
item.description:
Migration Request:

"Explain the difference between the 'Send to Production' and 'Send to Development' options when adding an Apple Push Certificate in Braze."

Knowledge Gap:

"Currently, Braze Docs may not clearly explain the distinction between sending push notifications through Apple's development gateway versus the production gateway. This information is essential for users to correctly configure push notifications for different app environments, ensuring that notifications are delivered as intended."

Recommended Actions:

"Add a section that details the purpose of the 'Send to Production' and 'Send to Development' options when configuring Apple Push Certificates. Explain that 'Send to Development' is used for apps built in development mode, which require push notifications to be sent through Apple's development gateway. Conversely, 'Send to Production' is for apps distributed via Apple's Test Flight or AppCenter, which should use the production gateway. Include guidance on selecting the appropriate option based on the app's distribution method."

Explanations for Actions (Optional):

"Clarify that the choice between development and production gateways depends on how the app is built and distributed. Development mode apps require the development gateway, while apps distributed for testing or production should use the production gateway to ensure proper notification delivery."

Flag Sensitive Information:

"No sensitive information identified in the provided content."

Recommended Braze Docs Page (Optional):

Push FAQ: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/push/ios_push_notifications/
item.summary: The Difference Between "Send to Production" and "Send to Development" When Adding an Apple Push Certificate

item.title: [BD-4424] Using Liquid If Statements with Catalog Items
item.description:
1. Migration Request:
    • Enhance the Braze documentation regarding catalog items to include information on using Liquid if statements with catalog items and selections.
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation does not address how to effectively use Liquid if statements with catalog items and selections, which is essential for users looking to implement conditional logic in their messaging.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation to include:
      • Using Liquid If Statements with Catalog Items:

To be able to use catalog items as a variable with liquid, you would need to first declare the catalog list before you are able to retrieve the variable to use with if statements:
Catalog Items

{% catalog_items Test-list %}
{% if items[0].first-item == true %}
Do this
{% else %}
Do that
{% endif %}
In some circumstances, the following liquid snippet may work as well:{% catalog_items Test-list %}

{% if items[0].first-item == true %}
Do this
{% else %}
Do that
{% endif %}

Catalog Selections
This example shows if custom attribute venue_name has more then 10 characters or less then 10 characters. If blank it will abort.

{% catalog_selection_items item-list selections %}

{% if items[0].venue_name.size > 10 %}
.....
{% elsif items[0].venue_name.size < 10 %}
....
{% else %}
{% abort_message(no venue_name) %}
{% endif %}
Pro tip is to use the blue + to insert catalog's liquid to prevent any mistake.

        • Explanations for Actions (optional):
          • By providing this additional information on using Liquid if statements with catalog items and selections, users will have a clearer understanding of how to implement conditional logic in their messaging, enhancing their ability to create personalized and dynamic content.
        • Flag Sensitive Information:
          • No sensitive information identified.
        • Related PQ:
          • n/a
        • Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
          • n/a
item.summary: Using Liquid If Statements with Catalog Items

item.title: [BD-4415] Add Existing Users to a Subscription Group
item.description:
1. Migration request: Just add clarity that in order to 'add existing users to a particular subscription group', this would be done via our /subscription/status/set REST API endpoint.
  2. Knowledge gap: I believe this support article was created because if you use the article title as search terms on the public docs, this endpoint article does not come up as a suggestion. We could benefit by making this use case much more straightforward.
  3. Recommended actions: Include language on the endpoint doc that explicitly mentions how this endpoint can be used when you want to update existing users. I know we already have language about how for new users, they should use /users/track.

item.title: [BD-4411] What Could be the Reason for Large Number of Uninstalls of an Application
item.description:
1. Migration request: Include the two other reasons that a client may see an increase in uninstalls for an app.
  2. Knowledge gap: Educate the client on very common reasons that their app may experience spikes in uninstall rates.
  3. Recommended actions: 
    1. include all information from the knowledge article.
      1. explain how to check for a specific message that may have lead to uninstalls
      2. explain how the presence of the push notification error 'BadDeviceToken' can indicate users are uninstalling the app. If they see this error in bulk around the same timeframe as the spike, this is generally a genuine report of uninstalls.
  4. Explanations for actions (optional): Clients get concerned when a spike in uninstalls is observed. They often wonder if there is a technical issue causing this, when in fact, there can be normal reasons this metric fluctuates.
  5. Flag sensitive information: n/a
  6. Related PQ: n/a
  7. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/analytics/tracking/uninstall_tracking/#why-am-i-suddenly-seeing-a-spike-in-uninstalls
item.summary: What Could be the Reason for Large Number of Uninstalls of an Application

item.title: [BD-4385] When is a user's account flagged as having uninstalled the app? When are they considered to have installed the app?
item.description:
This article explains how/ when a user uninstalls and reinstalls an app.

"Uninstall tracking "tags" users who have uninstalled the app. In the case a client uses the "has not uninstalled" filter for a campaign, these tagged users will not receive the campaign.

If a user reinstalls the app and then simply leaves the app on their device, they will still have the uninstall tag. The tag is removed once the user starts a new session."
item.summary: When is a user's account flagged as having uninstalled the app? When are they considered to have installed the app?

item.title: [BD-4350] Identify Endpoint: User Alias not associated successfully with external ID after 201 response
item.description:
Migration request: {}Warn users of unexpected results when User Alias not associated successfully with external ID after 201 response using Braze REST endpoint  /users/identify

Knowledge gap: Our docs don’t mention unexpected results when User Alias not associated successfully with external ID after 201 response using Braze REST endpoint  /users/identify. The user alias is case-sensitive and the alias_name field must match the capitalization of the alias. If not, the request will return as 201 Success (if the call is in the correct syntax), but the alias-only user and the external ID will not be merged.

Recommended actions: Add a section provide information on unexpected results when User Alias not associated successfully with external ID after 201 response using Braze REST endpoint  /users/identify. For example:

A user with no external ID has an assigned user alias "JimJones@example.com" with the label set to "email". A request is made to our users/identify endpoint to assign an external ID to this user with the request body containing:

"alias_name:" "jimjones@example.com"
"alias_label:" "email"

Despite returning 201, the request will not assign the given external_id because the "J"s are case-sensitive. There is no user with the alias jimjones@example.com only JimJones@example.com

Flag sensitive information: There is sensitive information in the KA as one of our Support reps used their email as an example.{}

Related PQ: N/A{}

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/api/endpoints/user_data/post_user_identify/#how-it-works
item.summary: Identify Endpoint: User Alias not associated successfully with external ID after 201 response

  2. Recommended actions: Add the below table to our docs:
Error Details
BAD_DEVICE_ID  "token import with 'device_id' must have a device_id less than 255 bytes and at least 8 bytes"
BAD_EMAIL_SUBSCRIPTION_STATE  "'email_subscribe' must be 'subscribed', 'unsubscribed', or 'opted_in'"
BAD_SUBSCRIPTION_GROUP_ARRAY  "'subscription_groups' attribute must be an array"
BAD_SUBSCRIPTION_GROUP_HASH "Each item in the 'subscription_groups' array must be a JSON object with key values 'subscription_group_id' and 'subscription_state'"
BAD_SUBSCRIPTION_GROUP_ID "'subscription_group_id' must be a valid subscription group UUID"
BAD_SUBSCRIPTION_GROUP_STATE "'subscription_state' for a subscription group must be one of 'subscribed' or 'unsubscribed'"
INVALID_ATTRIBUTE_EMAIL_SUBSCRIPTION_INFO  "'email_subscription_info' is not a valid attribute"
BAD_LOCATION_UPDATE "'current_location' must be an object containing 'longitude' and 'latitude'"
BAD_PUSH_SUBSCRIPTION_STATE "'push_subscribe' must be 'subscribed', 'unsubscribed', or 'opted_in'"
BAD_PUSH_TOKEN_APP_ID "token import 'app_id' must be a valid app identifier from this app group"
BAD_PUSH_TOKEN_IMPORT "token import must have tokens and exclude 'external_id' and 'braze_id'"
BAD_PUSH_TOKEN_STRING "token import 'token' must be a String"
BAD_PUSH_TOKEN_VALUE "'push_tokens' must be an array of objects"
BLACKLISTED_EXTERNAL_USER_ID "The provided external_id is blacklisted and disallowed"
EMAIL_BAD_FORMAT "The value provided for the 'email' field is not a valid email."
EXTERNAL_USER_ID_TOO_LARGE_MSG "'external_id' must be fewer than 988 bytes"
INVALID_ALIAS "'alias_name' must be a String"
INVALID_ALIAS_LABEL "'alias_label' must be a String"
INVALID_ALIAS_OBJECT "alias objects must be a hash"
INVALID_APP_ID "app_id is invalid"
INVALID_ARRAY_ELEMENT_TYPES "Arrays cannot contain a mix of objects and scalars"
INVALID_BRAZE_ID "'braze_id' is invalid"
INVALID_EVENT_NAME "Invalid 'name' field"
INVALID_PROPERTIES "Invalid 'properties' field"
INVALID_PROPERTIES_BAD_KEY "[INVALID_PROPERTIES] - bad key '[key]' in 'properties'"
INVALID_PROPERTIES_BAD_VALUE "[INVALID_PROPERTIES] - bad value for key '[key]' in 'properties' (cannot be arrays or other objects)"
INVALID_PROPERTIES_NON_STRING_KEY "[INVALID_PROPERTIES] - key '[key]' in 'properties' must be a string"
INVALID_PROPERTIES_OBJECT "[INVALID_PROPERTIES] - 'properties' must be an object"
 
INVALID_PROPERTIES_TOO_BIG "[INVALID_PROPERTIES] - the size of all event properties must be less than 50 kb in size"
INVALID_PURCHASE_QUANTITY "'quantity' is not valid"
INVALID_PURCHASE_VALUE "the total value of this purchase is too large"
MISSING_ATTRIBUTE_ASSIGNMENT "no attributes provided"
MISSING_EVENT_NAME "'name' is required"
MISSING_IDENTIFIER_ERROR "'external_id' or 'braze_id' or 'user_alias' is required"
MISSING_OR_INVALID_CURRENCY "'currency' must be an ISO 4217 currency"
MISSING_OR_INVALID_PRICE "'price' is not valid"
MISSING_OR_INVALID_TIME "'time' must be an ISO 8601 timestamp"
MISSING_PRODUCT_ID "'product_id' is required"
MORE_THAN_ONE_IDENTIFIER_ERROR "You must specify only one of 'external_id', 'user_alias' or 'braze_id'"
UNKNOWN_LANGUAGE_FORMAT_STRING "'language' must be an ISO-639 two-letter code, received [language]"
  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/api/endpoints/user_data/post_user_track/#monthly-active-users-cy-24-25

item.title: [BD-4307] Slowness in Displaying Action-Based Content Cards
item.description:
1. Migration request: I was kind of mixed in making this request because it may be possible this is outdated due to the introduction of the "At First Impression" feature for Content Cards. That being said we do get the occasional ticket for this and it may cut sown on those if this information is within our docs.
    2. Knowledge gap: Action Based content delivery for campaigns is not instant and its expected.
    3. Recommended actions: From the KA:
      For action-based delivery campaigns(like session start), it is expected that there will be a short but indeterminate delay between the event and the card being available. For better understanding, the backend process for content cards triggered by session start is: 
      • The campaign gets triggered by session start
      • Content card is created in database for that user
      • SDK performs a content card sync and will pull all available cards to the deviceThis means that the session start event needs to be flushed to our servers first then trigger the campaign. After the campaign gets triggered, it takes time for the content card to get created and then, there needs to be a sync to get all the available content cards. 

The alternate solution for this to mitigate the delay is to use Scheduled Delivery instead of Action-Based Delivery. However, the concern of using Scheduled Delivery is that new users are not able to receive these cards, as these users did not exist in Braze when the cards were sent. New users can only receive action triggered cards on the first session after a few seconds delay. 

In summary, the short delay is inevitable for new users in their first session, but it can be avoided for existing users with scheduled content cards. Thus, in this approach, you will have to duplicate all cards in 2 campaigns, a scheduled one for all existing users (i.e. session count > 0) and an action-triggered one to target new users (i.e. session count = 0). 
We may need to add some or even all of this information on the below page. 

    1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/content_cards/create/?tab=campaign#step-6-review-and-deploy
item.summary: Slowness in Displaying Action-Based Content Cards

item.title: [BD-4304] API Success Message Received But No Message Delivered
item.description:
1. Migration request: Add a bit more context to our docs about what a success message received for a REST API request means.
  2. Knowledge gap: We mention in our docs that a success message from a messaging endpoint just means the call was received for processing and why this doesn't guarantee delivery of messages. What we don't mention is that for endpoints like /users/identify, the success message just means that the call was formatted correctly, and not necessarily that the information was processed as expected.
  3. Recommended actions: Add the following context to the docs at the link below:

For example, for /users/identify, if the request synax and format is correct, it will return a success message. This means that Braze has received the request for processing. However, after processing, if there is no match for the alias, it will be dropped.

  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/api/errors/#server-responses
item.summary: API Success Message Received But No Message Delivered

item.title: [BD-4299] Multiple IAMs Display In The Same Session if They Are Both Triggered by 'Session Start'?
item.description:
1. Migration request: Clarify the end-user experience of triggering multiple IAMs from one event, and how Braze uses priority and recency to determine the order of the IAMs to be displayed to that user.
  2. Knowledge gap: Our docs don't mention that multiple IAMs won't display for the user in the same session. We state that we display based on priority, but we should clarify that this requires the user to log a new session to get the next IAM in the priority list.
  3. Recommended actions: 
    1. Answer this question: Can two or more IAMs display in the same session if they both trigger off of 'Session Start'? Which is "no", one IAM per session.
    2. Further explain how priority and recency of the IAM being created will play into the order of the IAMs being displayed for the user.
      1. In other words, take the example about 3 medium priority IAMs and add that to the article. 
  4. Explanations for actions (optional): Our clients like to be very explicit in understanding what the final user experience is going to be. In this case, we haven't made it super clear that the multiple IAMs a user triggers/is elgible for, won't display in the same session. Instead, the user will need to log future sessions in order to receive the next priority IAM.
  5. Flag sensitive information: 
  6. Related PQ: 
  7. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): "choose a priority" section of https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/in-app_messages/traditional/create/#step-7-build-the-remainder-of-your-campaign-or-canvas
    1. Alternatively, consider creating a separate doc that has examples for expected display behavior when a client has multiple IAM campaigns with the same trigger (ie session start).
item.summary: Multiple IAMs Display In The Same Session if They Are Both Triggered by 'Session Start'?

item.title: [BD-4298] Testing Content Cards in an iOS App
item.description:
1. Migration request: Provide extra details around why testing a Content Card requires the test user to be enabled/opted in to Push Notifications. 
  2. Knowledge gap: Clients are commonly confused why they need to be push enabled to test a content card. 
  3. Recommended actions: 
    1. The 'Test Checklist' should have a bullet point for 'ensure your test user is opted into push with a valid push token'.
    2. 'Debug' should include a screenshot of the system warning you the test content card can't be sent because they user isn't enabled for push (see image attached). Clarify again, and link to the above 'test' callout, that your test user must be enabled for push to receive the card.
    3. Include the detail that Test content cards typically expire after ~5 minutes (assuming this is still valid based on Product confirmation).
    4. Potentially, provide extra technical context for why this Push notification is needed:
      1. "To view a test Content Card (sent using the "Test" feature in the Braze dashboard), we package the card within a Push payload, and when the SDK opens the push, it finds the card in the payload and adds it to the locally cached cards.

The reason behind this is that a "Test" send avoids the normal live send system where we add the card to the database that shows which cards are available for which users."

  1. Explanations for actions (optional): 
  2. Flag sensitive information: 
  3. Related PQ: 
  4. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):  https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/content_cards/testing/#test
item.summary: Testing Content Cards in an iOS App

item.title: [BD-4281] How to find an anonymous user in the Web SDK
item.description:
Migration request: Provide customer ways to find an anonymous user in the Web SDK.

Knowledge gap: Our docs do not mention how to find anonymous user in the Web SDK.

Recommended actions: Add a section that show customers how to find an anonymous user in the Web SDK. 

In a web browser go to In a web browser's developer tools. For example, in Google Chrome, go to View > Developer > Developer tools or use short-cut Command + Option + J on a Mac or Ctrl + Shift + I or press F12 on Windows.

Enter the below into the Console tab in the browser's Developer Tools (switching appboy for braze for SDK versions below 4.0)

braze.getDeviceId(function(id){console.log(id)})

Or you can:

  1. Open console. 
  2. Click on “Network”
  3. Type in “data/“ in the search field.
  4. Select data/ under Name to find device Id

Flag sensitive information: In the screenshots of the KA, there is no sensitive information.{}

Related PQ: N/A{}

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): Maybe add an FAQs section here  ** https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/home
item.summary: How to find an anonymous user in the Web SDK

item.title: [BD-4278] Limitations for Catalog
item.description:
1. Migration request: We need to migrate some missing information and change the format for the limitations on the Catalogs. We might benefit from making a table. 
  2. Knowledge gap: Here is what the KA has and what should be moved over and added to a table.
CSV file size Up to 100 MB for all CSV files combined across your company Up to 2 GB for a single CSV file
Characters limit for item value Up to 5,000 characters in one value. For example, if you had a field labeled description, the maximum number of characters within the field is 5,000. Up to 5,000 characters in one value. For example, if you had a field labeled description, the maximum number of characters within the field is 5,000.
Characters limit for item column name Up to 250 characters Up to 250 characters
Selections Up to 30 selections per catalog Up to 30 selections per catalog

Note: Another known limitation in Catalogs is the use of Catalog Liquid tags recursively inside catalogs. You cannot call a Catalog item that then calls a second catalog and catalog item.

  1. Recommended actions: Add this to the following Docs section:

item.title: [BD-4269] Most Recently Used Device Feature For Push Messaging
item.description:
Migration request: Add clarity for how the "Most Recently Used Device" feature works

Knowledge gap: The public docs mention this feature and how it works. But, the Knowledge Article has an additional point that is missing from our public docs - which device will Braze target? Is "most recently used device" defined by sessions, or the timestamp when the push token was imported?

Recommended actions: Update the public docs to include this information from the Knowledge Article:

  • "Enabling this feature will target push delivery for only one device, based on which device has the most recently updated Push token. It does not target the device with the most recently recorded session. For example, if a new device with a push token is added to a user via API, the new device is considered the user's "most recently used device". This is true even if the user has not logged a session on that new device before."

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): On the "Creating a Push Message" page, we should add this in the "Device options" section https://braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/push/creating_a_push_message#device-options
item.summary: Most Recently Used Device Feature For Push Messaging

item.title: [BD-4247] Setting Custom Attribute as "" (blank) vs. null
item.description:
Description:
This article explains the difference between setting a Custom Attribute as an empty string ("") versus null in Braze. It highlights the implications of each choice based on the data type of the attribute and provides examples to clarify the correct usage.

Migration request:
We would like to migrate this content to Braze Docs to ensure users understand the distinction between using "" (blank) and null when setting Custom Attributes, particularly for Boolean data types. This is crucial for preventing errors in user data management.

Knowledge gap:
Currently, Braze Docs do not clearly articulate the consequences of using "" versus null for Custom Attributes, especially for Boolean types. This information is important as it can prevent users from encountering issues where attributes do not behave as expected, leading to confusion and potential data integrity problems.

Recommended actions:

  • Add a section that clearly explains the difference between setting Custom Attributes as "" and null, with a focus on Boolean data types.
  • Include examples demonstrating the correct usage of null for Boolean attributes and the implications of using an empty string.
  • Clarify that attributes set to "" will appear in user profiles as empty values, while those set to null will not be listed at all.

Flag sensitive information:
There is no sensitive information in this article that needs to be flagged for internal use only.

Related PQ:
n/a

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
Consider integrating this content into the following Braze Docs page: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/data/custom_data/custom_attributes
item.summary: Setting Custom Attribute as "" (blank) vs. null

item.title: [BD-4234] How do I use the spacer.gif resource?
item.description:
Knowledge gap : the use of the spacer.gif in webhooks for a canvas step is not mentioned in our docs.

 

Recommended actions :

Add the following into the Canvas documentation for a webhook step :

"We have a hack of our webhooks by calling a resource we host called spacer.gif. The spacer.gif image will not be sent or displayed to any users, but the webhook call will be successfully sent. This acts as a "dummy" step in canvas, so everyone technically gets it but no one actually receives or sees the message.

  1. Select Webhook as your message channel
  1. Select Blank Template
  1. Enter in http://braze-images.com/spacer.gif in the Webhook URL field
  1. Go to Settings (gear icon in upper right), and select POST as HTTP Method.

This will actually do nothing, and enable you to move users straight on in the Canvas journey and separate the audience on the first (but actually it's the second) step."

Docs link : https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/webhooks/creating_a_webhook/?tab=canvas#step-1-choose-where-to-build-your-message
item.summary: How do I use the spacer.gif resource?

comments.comment: Would love to get Product's pov on this , if we should make it public. Seem like we have 39 case where this was a solution consideration? Is this something we actively suggest during onboarding (or post-sales guidance)?

item.title: [BD-4225] Test Push With Users That Have Multiple Devices
item.description:
Description:
This ticket addresses the need to clarify the behavior of the test push feature in Braze when users have multiple devices. The goal is to ensure users understand how test pushes are sent and the options available to control this behavior.

Migration request:
We would like to migrate content that explains how the test push function operates for users with multiple devices. For example, “Clarify whether users can expect to receive test pushes on all devices or only the most recently used device.”

Knowledge gap:
Currently, there may be confusion among users regarding the behavior of test pushes when they have multiple devices linked to their profile. Understanding this functionality is important for users to manage their testing effectively and avoid unintended notifications across all devices. This information can enhance user experience by providing clarity on how to control push notifications during testing.

Recommended actions:
Add a section that clearly outlines how the test push feature works for users with multiple devices. This should include:

  • An explanation that the test push will be sent to all push-enabled devices associated with the user’s profile.
  • Information on how users can specify that the push should only be sent to the most recently used device.
  • Examples of scenarios where this functionality is relevant, such as testing campaigns across different devices.

Flag sensitive information:
There is no sensitive information in the current content that needs to be flagged for internal use only.

Related PQ:
If there are any related Product Questions (PQ) that provide additional context for this migration, please include links here.

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
To add Push FAQ section here: https://www.braze.com/docs/help/faqs
item.summary: Test Push With Users That Have Multiple Devices

item.title: [BD-4203] What is the Difference Between a Full Sync and a Partial Sync for Content Cards?
item.description:
1. Migration request: Provide more information about full sync vs partial sync for content card refresh.
  2. Knowledge gap: Braze docs does not currently have this information, although it is available in this article: https://www.braze.com/resources/articles/9-tips-and-tricks-for-braze-content-cards#:~:text=%236%3A%20Get%20Familiar%20with%20Your%20Syncing%20Options 
  3. Recommended actions: Under the suggested docs page, the following can be added as a paragraph: 
    "Here are two syncing options available within Braze when you’re working with Content Cards:
    • Full Sync: Each time the changeUser() method is called (or every 7 days), Braze will perform a Full Sync by default and this pulls all of the Content Cards a given user is eligible for from our server.
    • Partial Sync: In comparison, Partial Syncs pull down only the new Content Cards the user is eligible for from our server. If a user isn't eligible for any new Content Cards, the request will return 0 cards. Partial syncs occur when requestContentCardsRefresh() is called.
      • Partial syncs limit the burden on our servers and the device's battery life. Content Cards once received are stored on the SDK locally so while Partial Syncs can return 0 Content Cards, the user will still see the available Content Cards they are eligible for."
  1. Explanations for actions (optional): N.A
  2. Flag sensitive information: Do not include any info under "Internal Notes"
  3. Related PQ: N.A.
  4. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/content_cards/customizing_cards/feed/#refreshing-the-feed **
item.summary: What is the Difference Between a Full Sync and a Partial Sync for Content Cards?

comments.comment:
Interestingly, we have some mention of it https://www.braze.com/resources/articles/9-tips-and-tricks-for-braze-content-cards

but yes, would be good to have it in the content card doc itself.

item.title: [BD-4174] How is a Content Card's Expiration (Time In Feed) Calculated?
item.description:
1. Migration request: Further elaborate how expiration time in calculated in Braze.
  2. Knowledge gap: Braze docs mentions expiration time for content cards, but does not explain how it is calculated. 
  3. Recommended actions: Add a paragraph at the bottom of the "Overview" section for the campaign tab: "Regardless of selected option, the Expiration (Time In Feed) is calculated from its Send Time.
    1. For a scheduled send, it will be the time after the campaign is launched.
    2. For action-based campaigns, it will be the time after the user performs the action. For example, if the card is sent at 1400 today and the expiration setting is 1 day, the card will expire at 1400 tomorrow."
  4. Explanations for actions (optional): N.A.
  5. Flag sensitive information: N.A.
  6. Related PQ: N.A.
  7. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/content_cards/create/card_creation/#overview:~:text=Regardless%20of%20your%20selected%20option%2C%20the%20Content%20Card%20expiration%20date%20countdown%20will%20begin%20when%20the%20campaign%20launches. **
item.summary: How is a Content Card's Expiration (Time In Feed) Calculated?

item.title: [BD-4173] Knowledge What is the Liquid for Templating in a Date/Time in the User's Local Time Zone?
item.description:
Description:
This article explains how to use Liquid for templating date and time in the user's local time zone. It provides examples of how to format dates and times using Liquid syntax, ensuring that users can effectively display time-sensitive information in their communications.

Migration request:
We would like to migrate this content to ensure that users understand how to render dates and times in their local time zones using Liquid. For example, “Explain how to format dates/times in the user's local time zone using Liquid syntax.”

Knowledge gap:
This article fills a knowledge gap in Braze Docs by providing clear guidance on how to use Liquid to display dates and times in the user's local time zone. Currently, our documentation does not adequately address how to handle time zone conversions, which is crucial for users who want to personalize their messaging based on the recipient's local time. This information is important as it enhances user experience by ensuring that time-sensitive notifications are accurate and relevant.

Recommended actions:

  • Add a section that explains the importance of displaying dates/times in the user's local time zone.
  • Include a brief summary of the Liquid syntax for time zone conversion and formatting.
  • Provide additional examples that demonstrate various use cases, such as displaying upcoming events or deadlines in the user's local time.

Explanations for actions (optional):
The need for this article was inspired by user inquiries about how to properly format dates and times in their communications. Users have expressed confusion regarding the default UTC rendering and how to convert it to local time. By addressing these questions directly in the documentation, we can help users avoid common pitfalls and enhance their understanding of Liquid.

Flag sensitive information:
There is no sensitive information in this article that should remain internal.

Related PQ:
N/A

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
We could add the additional examples under the time zones section in the Liquid use case library article:
https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/personalization_and_dynamic_content/liquid/liquid_use_cases
item.summary: Knowledge What is the Liquid for Templating in a Date/Time in the User's Local Time Zone?

item.title: [BD-4114] Missing Stats for Content Cards Campaigns
item.description:

  2. Knowledge gap: Braze docs does not cover this topic.
  3. Recommended actions: In the IAM troubleshooting page for each platform (example, Web), include another section that mentions "Missing Content Cards Analytics". Where the content could be: "If the Content Cards are displayed correctly in your app and you are consistently not receiving any analytics (unique recipients, impressions, clicks, etc.), it is likely an SDK integration issue."  With the following details for Android, iOS: "If you are using a Custom Content Card View, note that you must implement the logging of Impressions and Clicks manually within your application. (Android, Swift, iOS)" For Web, the details could be "The default Content Card integration is Braze's display module. When this is integrated correctly, it automatically tracks the events related to content cards displaying. Alternatively, if this has been implemented with manual handling/customization, each of the analytics events need to be logged though logContentCardClick, logContentCardImpressions, etc. For a Web SDK version lower than 4.6.0, please refer to the specific version of our JS documentation for the correct method"
  4. Explanations for actions (optional): So users can potentially self-help the issue before raising a Support case
  5. Flag sensitive information: Do not include the Confluence Article under the Internal Tools section

item.title: [BD-4080] Formatting for DateTime datatype?
item.description:
1. Migration request: We don't have all the information within the article in our docs.
  2. Knowledge gap: We don't have all the information within the article in our docs.
  3. Recommended actions: This is what is missing:

Keep in mind that if not setting a timezone, or setting timezone incorrectly, we will always default to UTC. Time will always appear in company timezone on the dashboard. So if you wanted to set the time for October 31, 2019 6pm EST it would look as follows:

2019-10-31T18:00:00+04:00

If data sent in the wrong format the date will display an incorrect value.

  1. Explanations for actions (optional): N/A
  2. Flag sensitive information: Not sensitive, but the screenshot should not be migrated.
  3. Related PQ: N/A
  4. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/api/objects_filters/user_attributes_object - under the Custom attribute data types > Dates

item.title: [BD-4078] What is the minimum time interval between triggers for IAMs?
item.description:
1. Migration request: There are some recomendations that are not available in our public docs.
  2. Knowledge gap: This information is missing and can improve their SDK integration.
  3. Recommended actions: 
    In production apps, we do not recommend making this value any smaller than 10, to avoid spamming the user with notifications. In our sample apps, we have reduced that time to 5 seconds.

Are there any downsides to setting the minimal time interval between triggers to 0?
You can reduce the interval below 5 seconds, but our suggestion is just to test at 0 seconds because if a user should receive both IAMs and they have not yet dismissed IAM 1, then IAM 2 isn't expected to trigger. The console logs would show Cannot show in-app message test because another message is being shown.That's the reasoning why the default is 30 seconds.

  1. Explanations for actions (optional): N/A
  2. Flag sensitive information: N/A
  3. Related PQ: N/A
  4. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): 
    https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/platforms/android/in_app_messages/delivery?redirected=2#minimum-time-interval-between-triggers
    https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/platforms/web/in_app_messages/delivery?redirected=2#minimum-time-interval-between-triggers
    https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/platforms/swift/in_app_messages/message_delivery/#delivery-semantics
item.summary: What is the minimum time interval between triggers for IAMs?

item.title: [BD-4075] ChangeUser() SDK method FAQ
item.description:
1. Migration request: Explains to customers the expected behaviour when using the SDK method ChangeUser()
  2. Knowledge gap: We do not have a concise place explaining about these behaviour and warning customers about what might happen if they do not use it right.
  3. Recommended actions: Add in our public docs, in the begining of their SDK integration, the details below.
  4. Explanations for actions (optional): 
    A few notes on how changeUser() method functions:
    • Calling changeUser() multiple times with the same user ID does not affect session count
    • changeUser() can also be called when an anonymous user creates a profile to assign them a user ID
      • If this user ID already exists, the anonymous user's data will not be merged into the identified profile
      • If calling changeUser() with a new user ID, the anonymous user's data will be merged into the new identified profile and a new session begins immediately
    • When you request a user switch (which is any call to changeUser where the new user ID is not the same as the existing user ID), the current session for the previous user (anonymous or not) is automatically ended and a new session is started.
    • You should not call changeUser() when the user logs out
    • You should not call changeUser() to a default profile, as this will prevent anonymous users and log all data to one external id, potentially causing other features to not behave as expected
  5. Flag sensitive information: N/A
  6. Related PQ: N/A
  7. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): I'm not sure the best doc to add these information, because this is about all SDKs (iOS, Android and Web).
item.summary: ChangeUser() SDK method FAQ

item.title: [BD-4068] User Profile Attribute "Language"
item.description:
1. Migration request: Inform users that a user profile's Language can be automatically set based on their device locale
  2. Knowledge gap: Braze docs currently does not have this information
  3. Recommended actions: Add a small section with the following information: "A user's profile language is based on Device Locale, which takes into account the language of the operating system on the device. For example, if a user lived in the U.S. and their device OS language was set to English, their device locale would be "en_US" and their language would be set to English ("en"). If they lived in the U.S. but their device OS language was set to Spanish, their device locale would be "es_US" and their language would be set to Spanish ("es").
    An exception to this is if the language attribute has ever been manually updated via REST API, which overrides the automatic setting of a user's language." 
  1. Explanations for actions (optional): N.A.
  2. Flag sensitive information: N.A.
  3. Related PQ: N.A
  4. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/data/user_data_collection/sdk_data_collection/#optional-data-collected-by-default or https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/engagement_tools/segments/user_profiles/#overview-tab **
item.summary: User Profile Attribute "Language"

item.title: [BD-4048] Deep Links on Android Not Working
item.description:
1. Migration request: This KA is a troubleshooting guide for clients to go through prior to reaching out to support. It would be great to have this guide in our public-facing docs. ** It might also be nice to have a section at the end of this guide that says "Still experiencing issues? Reach out to support here" with a link to create a case with support. 
  1. Knowledge gap: Our docs do contain information about deep links for Android however on that page there is no (To my knowledge) troubleshooting guide for when things do not function as they should.
  2. Recommended actions: We should add the following steps for clients to work through issues with their Deeplinks involving android devices. This way when they come to support we can find the issue faster:

From the KA: 
Before escalating your issues to the Braze Support team, there are a couple of things you could check to troubleshoot the deeplink issue you're encountering on Android. We recommend working with your Android developers in going through these steps:

    • Check if the deeplink works when opened on other apps. If you open the deeplink using Slack, Notes or etc, does it open your app and redirect to the correct section of your app? If it does not, it is likely that your deeplink is not configured correctly. Kindly work with your developers in implementing/creating deeplinks with your app following this Android documentation. 
       
    • If the issue is that clicking the push notification with a deeplink does not open the app, check if you have enabled Braze to automatically open your app and any deep links when a push notification is clicked.
      • Documentation
      • File to check: braze.xml
      • Code to find/check: 
<bool name="com_braze_handle_push_deep_links_automatically">true</bool>
      • Please note that this can also be set via runtime configuration.
      • If this is not set, you should set it to true to enable Braze to automatically open your app and any deep links when a push notification is clicked.
         
    • If the issue is that the on-click behavior of the message does not result in the deeplink to redirect correctly. Here are things to look out for:
      1. If the link is sent via multiple channels (such as in-app message and push) does it work in either?
      2. Confirm whether you have implemented the IBrazeDeeplinkHandler. In most cases, the delegate is set in the app's Application.onCreate(). 
        • Documentation
        • File to check: Main Application class
        • Code to find: 
BrazeDeeplinkHandler.setBrazeDeeplinkHandler()
        • If a custom BrazeDeeplinkHandler has been implemented, kindly check if you are handling the links correctly. You can add breakpoints in your code to debug where in your implementation could be causing problems. You can also refer to the default IBrazeDeeplinkHander of the Braze Android SDK. 
      1. Check the verbose logs for messages that include the deep link URL contained in the message. 
        • File to check: verbose logs and AndroidManifest.xml
        • Logs to find: Could not find appropriate activity to open for deep link <deeplink URL>
        • If you see this in the verbose logs, this indicates that the deeplink URL is not registered. 
Check the AndroidManifest.xml and verify the Deep Link Configuration. If the deeplink is example://test, you should see the <data android:scheme="example" android:host="test" /> in the AndroidManifest.xml: 
<activity android:name=".SampleActivity">
    <intent-filter>
        <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
        <category android:name="android.intent.category.DEFAULT" />
        <category android:name="android.intent.category.BROWSABLE" />
        <data android:scheme="example" android:host="test" />
    </intent-filter>
</activity>

If you cannot find an intent filter that contains the deeplink, kindly work with your developers in implementing/creating deeplinks with your app following this Android documentation. 
 

    • Lastly, you can test your deep links following this Android documentation.

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): Add it to the following Docs page (Towards the bottom) Or possibly create a Troubleshooting dedicated page and link it to this page: https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/platforms/android/push_notifications/deep_linking/#prerequisites

Note: There's a paragraph mentioning Verbose logs, it might be good to Hyper link over to the Verbose/Braze Logging docs we have.
item.summary: Deep Links on Android Not Working

comments.comment: May be good to get SDK team to take a look and see how much detail can we go thru in our public docs. have a list of pre-reqs to submit to support would be great. May be SDK debugger can address some of this and we could indicate that in the troubleshooting section?

item.title: [BD-3994] Adding common troubleshooting steps for Logging Custom Events
item.description:
Request from Rory Prutton(rory.prutton@braze.com)

  • Request_Type
    • standard
  • Description
    • There isn't any information which we can give the customer for generic troubleshooting which is ambiguous of the specific SDK (as they all generally follow the same rules)

I.e.
1. Custom event logging was not set up correctly by the customer developer team.
--> You should reach out to your developer team, and have the dev team ensure that the custom event is being logged correctly. If the developer is having trouble identifying the issue with the custom event, the developer can log a support ticket with Braze. From there, the support team can assist with the custom event logging setup. You can also reach out to your dev team to ensure that you are correctly triggering the custom event.

2. The customer is not performing the correct action to log the Braze custom event (for example: the user is clicking the wrong button).
--> General pitfalls are deprecated code, where they are using appboy instead of braze after upgrading the SDK.

3. The user may not be identified (via changeUser() method) and the custom events are logged towards an anonymous profile.
--> Calling changeUser() before performing the custom event should ensure that the custom event is logged towards the identified user profile

We can also here (perhaps under the first heading) ask customers to get verbose logs and code snippets when they raise to support

  • Request_Url
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/help/home
item.summary: Adding common troubleshooting steps for Logging Custom Events

comments.comment: KA link -https://braze.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka0VP000000I1YjYAK/view

Hi Rory Prutton , for #2, the solution doesn't seem connected to the problem. If the user is clicking the wrong button, wouldn't the fix be to check the custom event and make sure that the custom event is linked to the correct button?

Or is the solution pointing to a different issue?
Rory Prutton quick reminder regarding Rachel's question above. Could you help confirm pls ? Thanks!

Hey Rachel Feinberg, apologies for the delay in response here.

To be honest #2 is a weak point as it's almost 'common sense' that if you click a button which doesn't log a custom event then the custom event will not be logged.

And for the solution it really entails who is checking. A marketer could easily try to click the button and see if it leads to the correct/incorrect custom event; whereas a developer would go the button itself to check which custom event has been added to the button.

I think just pointing out that a button might not have the custom event in question connected would be enough to go off really, I say this with experience that I have never encountered the issue to be linked to the problem involved in #2

item.title: [BD-3985] Adding more information on syntax for boolean
item.description:
Request from Alan Zaki(alan.zaki@braze.com)

  • Request_Type
    • standard
  • Description
    • I wonder if we can add a section where we specify that the data type of the custom attribute being called in the Liquid matches the data type of the attribute in the dashboard with the example below:

Let's say the following Liquid conditional statement is in your campaign message body, but when you preview/test your message body using a custom user and add the custom attribute value of true, the message is not populating as expected.

{% if {{custom_attribute.$

{newsletter_subscribed}}} == 'true' %}

The data type used in the syntax of the conditional statement does not match the data type of the custom attribute being called in the Liquid.

Check the data type of the custom attribute being called in the Liquid and make sure the syntax of the conditional statement matches the data type of the attribute, in Manage Settings > Custom Attributes.

For example, if your Liquid syntax is the below, the custom attribute data type should be a string in order for the Liquid to function properly.
{% if {{custom_attribute.${newsletter_subscribed}

}} == 'true' %}
If the custom attribute data type was a boolean, the Liquid statement would need to look like the below (with quotation marks removed) in order for it to work properly, as a boolean attribute has boolean values of true or false.
{% if {{custom_attribute.$

{newsletter_subscribed}

}} == true %}

  • Request_Url
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/data/custom_data/custom_attributes/#managing-custom-attributes
item.summary: Adding more information on syntax for boolean

item.title: [BD-3965] adding more information on example for data consumption
item.description:
Request from Alan Zaki(alan.zaki@braze.com)

  • Request_Type
    • standard
  • Description
    • could we add additional information to the note maybe with an example: Updating a custom attribute object to null also consumes a data point.

ADD:
assuming I've got an array:
array_attribute: ["value1", "value2"]

array_attribute :

{ remove : [ "Value3" ]}

If I remove a value that does not exist, does it incur DP?
array_attribute :

{ remove : [ "Value1" ]}

If I remove a value that exist, does it incur DP?
Let's say if I run REST API - user/track request with custom_attribute_name : null information, is this request also consume the DP?
If com.appboy.enable_location_collection is set to true or yes, the profile data 'Most Recent Device Locale' will be automatically collected by Braze every time when users start session.
Then, will this automatic collection of 'Most Recent Device Locale' be counted toward datapoint consumption as follows or not?
Answer:

Any update to a Custom Attribute will be counted as a data point, including passing a null value, this includes if the value is already a null value.
In the case of an array a data point is consumed regardless of whether the element exists in the array or not as we are attributing the data point to our processing of the data.

Also for #3, 1 datapoint will be consumed for a session start and 1 datapoint for Most Recent Device Locale. So in that example it would be 2 in total.
When an empty value is passed (e.g., a custom attribute is created but has no associated value), it would still consume data points. Whenever Braze sets (or unset) a user's custom attribute value to anything, it consumes a data point.

Do note that datapoints will be consumed even if the payload has unchanging data as per our documentation

  • Request_Url
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/data/data_points?redirected=2#consumption-count
item.summary: adding more information on example for data consumption

comments.comment: KA - https://braze.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka0VP000000HImvYAG/view

Alan Zaki - Looks like your description is already included in the doc. 

Were you suggesting that we add more info? What would that be? Thank you!
Manjula Karunakaran I was referring to adding the examples and more information than the line added in the Note: section
Thanks Alan! Joshua McCrowell - I am not sure on what our guidelines are reg providing examples when do we provide vs when we do not. Would you have any guidance ? Thanks!

I don't know if there are guidelines per se–this is part of the work of curating the docs and making the articles up to date, relevant, searchable, findable, etc. 

I admit, I'm having some trouble parsing the KB article. I don't exactly understand the nuance here from the KB article itself. 

In its current state, I don't think the request to pull this exact text from the KB article into this docs page should go forward. The actual information that the KB article is trying to communicate needs to be pulled out and rearticulated. Just moving the KB article's examples into the documentation would harm the quality of the documentation, as it is not up to our standards.
